title: quercus × numantina
intro: Quercus × numantina, also known as the hybrid of Quercus ilex and Quercus faginea, is a deciduous oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The bark and leaves of this tree have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus × numantina are believed to offer various health benefits, including protection against cardiovascular disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders, as well as antimicrobial and antiviral effects. Quercus × numantina is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and can also be used as a topical application in the form of a salve or cream.
